title: Frog Position After T Seconds
hints: Model the frog's movement as a random walk on the tree, tracking the probability of being at each node at each second.
Use dynamic programming where state is (current_node, time), updating probabilities based on the number of unvisited neighbors at each step.
Since the path is unique in a tree, the probability to reach a node is the product of inverse degrees of ancestors, adjusted for stopping conditions.
